beforeHooks.title: "before all" hook in "User Response Schema"
beforeHooks.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema "before all" hook in "User Response Schema"
beforeHooks.code:
const response = await axios.get(`${BASE_URL}/users/1`);
user = response.data;

tests.title: ✅ User object should have all required fields
tests.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema ✅ User object should have all required fields

tests.title: ✅ Field types should be correct
tests.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema ✅ Field types should be correct

tests.title: ✅ Nested address object structure should be correct
tests.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema ✅ Nested address object structure should be correct

tests.title: ✅ Geo coordinates structure should be correct
tests.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema ✅ Geo coordinates structure should be correct
tests.code:
expect(user.address.geo).to.have.property('lat').that.is.a('string');
expect(user.address.geo).to.have.property('lng').that.is.a('string');

tests.title: ✅ Company object structure should be correct
tests.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema ✅ Company object structure should be correct

tests.title: ✅ Email format should be valid (Regex check)
tests.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema ✅ Email format should be valid (Regex check)
tests.code:
const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
expect(user.email).to.match(emailRegex);

tests.title: ✅ id should be a positive integer
tests.fullTitle: 📐 Schema Validation Tests User Response Schema ✅ id should be a positive integer
